name: Assembly
creator: Various
description: Low-level language with a strong correspondence to machine code instructions.

name: Fortran
creator: John Backus / IBM
description: First high-level programming language, designed for scientific and numeric computation.

name: COBOL
creator: Grace Hopper / CODASYL
description: Business-oriented language widely used in legacy enterprise and financial systems.

name: Lisp
creator: John McCarthy
description: One of the oldest high-level languages, featuring symbolic expression processing and homoiconicity.

name: BASIC
creator: John Kemeny and Thomas Kurtz
description: Beginner-oriented language designed for ease of use on early personal computers.

name: Prolog
creator: Alain Colmerauer
description: Logic programming language based on formal logic, used for AI and constraint solving.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: Low-level systems programming language offering fine-grained memory control.

name: Pascal
creator: Niklaus Wirth
description: Structured language designed for teaching good programming practices.

name: Scheme
creator: Guy L. Steele and Gerald Jay Sussman
description: Minimalist Lisp dialect emphasizing proper tail recursion and first-class continuations.

name: SQL
creator: Donald D. Chamberlin and Raymond F. Boyce
description: Declarative language for managing and querying relational databases.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: Extension of C adding object-oriented features, templates, and the STL.

name: Objective-C
creator: Brad Cox and Tom Love
description: Superset of C with Smalltalk-style messaging, formerly primary language for Apple platforms.

name: Ada
creator: Jean Ichbiah / US DoD
description: Strongly typed language designed for safety-critical and real-time systems.

name: Perl
creator: Larry Wall
description: High-level scripting language known for powerful text processing and CPAN ecosystem.

name: Erlang
creator: Joe Armstrong / Ericsson
description: Functional language built for massively concurrent, fault-tolerant distributed systems.

name: Haskell
creator: A committee chaired by Simon Peyton Jones
description: Purely functional language with a strong static type system and lazy evaluation.

name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: General-purpose language emphasizing readability and simplicity.

name: Lua
creator: Roberto Ierusalimschy, Luiz Henrique de Figueiredo, Waldemar Celes
description: Lightweight embeddable scripting language with a simple C API.

name: R
creator: Ross Ihaka and Robert Gentleman
description: Language and environment for statistical computing and data visualization.

name: Ruby
creator: Yukihiro Matsumoto
description: Expressive, object-oriented language designed for developer happiness.

name: Java
creator: James Gosling / Sun Microsystems
description: Object-oriented language running on the JVM, emphasizing write-once-run-anywhere portability.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich / Netscape
description: The scripting language of the web, now used on both client and server via Node.js.

name: PHP
creator: Rasmus Lerdorf
description: Server-side scripting language widely used for web development.

name: OCaml
creator: INRIA
description: Functional language with a powerful type inference system and native-code compilation.

name: Racket
creator: Matthias Felleisen et al.
description: Lisp-family language designed as a platform for language creation and teaching.

name: C#
creator: Anders Hejlsberg / Microsoft
description: Modern, multi-paradigm language for the .NET ecosystem with strong tooling support.

name: Groovy
creator: James Strachan
description: JVM-based dynamic language with concise syntax and seamless Java interoperability.

name: Scala
creator: Martin Odersky
description: JVM language blending functional and object-oriented programming with a powerful type system.

name: F#
creator: Don Syme / Microsoft Research
description: Functional-first language for the .NET platform with type inference and immutability defaults.

name: PowerShell
creator: Jeffrey Snover / Microsoft
description: Task automation and configuration management shell built on the .NET runtime.

name: Clojure
creator: Rich Hickey
description: Modern Lisp for the JVM emphasizing immutable data and concurrency via STM.

name: Go
creator: Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, Ken Thompson / Google
description: Statically typed, compiled language with built-in concurrency primitives and simple syntax.

name: Rust
creator: Graydon Hoare / Mozilla
description: Systems language guaranteeing memory safety without a garbage collector via ownership.

name: Kotlin
creator: JetBrains
description: Modern JVM language with null safety, coroutines, and full Java interoperability.

name: Elixir
creator: José Valim
description: Functional language built on the Erlang VM for scalable and fault-tolerant applications.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg / Microsoft
description: Typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript, adding static type checking.

name: Julia
creator: Jeff Bezanson, Stefan Karpinski, Viral B. Shah, Alan Edelman
description: High-performance language for numerical and scientific computing with JIT compilation.

name: Swift
creator: Chris Lattner / Apple
description: Modern compiled language for Apple platforms with safety, speed, and expressive syntax.

name: Nim
creator: Andreas Rumpf
description: Statically typed compiled language combining Python-like syntax with C-like performance.

name: Dart
creator: Lars Bak and Kasper Lund / Google
description: Client-optimized language for fast apps on multiple platforms, best known for Flutter.

name: Crystal
creator: Ary Borenszweig and Juan Wajnerman
description: Ruby-inspired language with static typing and native compilation for high performance.

name: Shell/Bash
creator: Brian Fox / GNU Project
description: Unix shell and command language used for scripting and automating system tasks.

name: MATLAB
creator: Cleve Moler / MathWorks
description: Numeric computing environment and language designed for matrix operations and simulations.

name: Solidity
creator: Gavin Wood / Ethereum
description: Contract-oriented language for writing smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain.

name: WebAssembly
creator: W3C WebAssembly Community Group
description: Binary instruction format for a stack-based virtual machine, enabling near-native performance in browsers.
use_cases: porting native code to the web

name: Zig
creator: Andrew Kelley
description: Systems language prioritizing simplicity, explicit control flow, and no hidden allocations.
